purpose: Convert validated Google/VK identities into one durable Medusa customer and safe storefront session.
success_outcome: Backend callback completion is replay-safe, collision-safe, session-establishing, rate-limited, and fixed-redirect only.
anti_goals: Do not implement storefront UI or cart merge.
Do not auto-link accounts by email.
Do not modify Medusa Core or write auth tables directly.

success_checks: One provider identity resolves/creates one durable customer and session.
Same-email cross-provider collision and duplicate callback leave no partial state.
Completion uses fixed redirect, sanitized failures, and bounded hashed-key rate limiting.
evidence_required: Sanitized logs, typecheck, lint, T3 human checkpoint, and recovery note.

stop_conditions: Supported Medusa workflow cannot create/link customer without direct core writes.
Session cannot be established without exposing a token to browser URL/storage.
Callback destination or account collision policy is ambiguous.
required_handoff: Report changed_files, commands_run, evidence, scope_compliance, and blockers_or_none.
Run /verify and /red-verify; record HUMAN_CHECKPOINT and ROLLBACK_RECOVERY_NOTE before T3 closure.
